name: Castillo de Sarmiento and Palencia Loop
name_original: Castillo de Sarmiento desde Villamuriel de Cerrato – circular por Palencia
description: Cycle 39.9 miles (64.1 km) through Palencia's Cerrato region, featuring gentle hills, cereal fields, and the historic Castillo de Sarmiento.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Venta De Baños, located in Palencia, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by agricultural fields and historical sites. The terrain generally features gentle undulations, offering routes suitable for various fitness levels. This area is known for its historical significance, including ancient structures, and provides a backdrop of rural scenery. Best road cycling routes around Venta De Baños The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the Cerrato Villages, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 1326 feet (404 meters) through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Sarmiento and Palencia Loop, a moderate 39.8 miles (64.1 km) path. This route offers views of the Castillo de Sarmiento and the city of Palencia, with an elevation gain of 713 feet (217 meters). Local road cyclists also love the Fuente Juan José Niño – Nice place for picnic loop from Soto de Cerrato, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Road cycling around Venta De Baños is defined by agricultural fields, historical landmarks, and gently rolling terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Venta De Baños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 290 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Venta De Baños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Venta De Baños?
A: The road cycling routes around Venta De Baños primarily feature agricultural fields and rural scenery. You can expect gently undulating terrain, which offers a mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging sections suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Venta De Baños area?
A: There are 16 road cycling routes available around Venta De Baños. These include 2 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to see while cycling near Venta De Baños?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical significance. While cycling, you might encounter various historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166121483/castillo-de-sarmiento-and-palencia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sarmiento and Palencia Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Castillo de Sarmiento. Other notable attractions in the broader area include the ancient Visigothic Basilica of San Juan de Baños, considered the oldest Christian monument in Spain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3166451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Grande de Palencia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Venta De Baños?
A: The road cycling routes in Venta De Baños are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the rural landscapes and the variety of routes available for different abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options from Venta De Baños?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33894771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Cevico de la Torre loop from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 91.5 km (56.9 miles) with an elevation gain of 641 meters, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or towns?
A: Absolutely. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166145784/tour-of-the-cerrato-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Cerrato Villages&lt;/a&gt; are designed to take you through the local countryside, connecting various villages and offering a glimpse into the regional life and architecture.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a more relaxed ride or a picnic stop?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Juan José Niño – Nice place for picnic loop from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This 45.2 km (28.1 miles) route leads through pleasant rural landscapes and is known for offering a nice spot for a picnic.

Q: What are some of the cultural highlights I might encounter near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the historical sites, you might pass through areas with local orchards and fields, reflecting the region's agricultural heritage. In nearby Palencia, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Palencia&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercado de Abastos de Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, offering a taste of local life.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of significant structures like bridges?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate views of local infrastructure. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Soto de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt; includes the Puente Mayor de Palencia, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5793615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Puentecillas&lt;/a&gt; is another notable bridge in the vicinity.

Q: What is the general elevation profile for road cycling in Venta De Baños?
A: The routes around Venta De Baños are characterized by gentle undulations rather than steep climbs. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166145784/tour-of-the-cerrato-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Cerrato Villages&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 404 meters (1326 feet) over 68.9 km, indicating a rolling landscape.
tips: Stone bridge from the 14th century that crosses the Carrión river on the way to Palencia with beautiful symmetrical arches.

Masonry bridge erected in the 16th century and renovated at the end of the 19th century. The Puente Mayor crosses the Carrión River in Palencia, connecting the city center with the Allende El Río neighborhood. Originally made up of seven semicircular arches and prismatic cutwaters, it was later extended to allow road traffic. It currently has semi-cylindrical pillars and cutwaters with machicolation-shaped finishes, providing a fortified appearance.

One of the best fortified castles in Spain. The Castillo de los Sarmiento has an extensive wall and keep. The castle, as seen today, was built in the 15th century, like many others, replacing an older castle dating back to the 10th century.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the "Castillo de Sarmiento from Villamuriel de Cerrato – circular by Palencia" route?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Villamuriel de Cerrato. The Diputación de Palencia is actively developing cycling infrastructure, and Villamuriel de Cerrato is designated as a starting point for new routes, which typically includes dedicated parking facilities for cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the El Cerrato region, characterized by undulating hills and plains. You can expect long distances with generally gentle slopes and shallow valleys, making it suitable for accumulating kilometers without overly demanding climbs. The roads are often well-maintained and have low traffic.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and summer offer particularly scenic experiences. In spring, the undulating hills are covered in poppies, while in summer, you'll see wide fields of cereal crops turning yellow. The region's low traffic also contributes to a pleasant cycling experience during these seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. The Palencia province actively promotes cycling tourism in the area.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally welcome in the region, having a dog run alongside a racebike on public roads is not recommended for safety reasons. If you wish to bring your dog, consider using a specialized bicycle trailer.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the historic Puente de Villamuriel de Cerrato and the Puente Mayor de Palencia. A key highlight is the 15th-century Castle of the Sarmiento in Fuentes de Valdepero, which offers spectacular views.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Cerrato region, where this route is located, is described as having gentle elevations and shallow valleys, making it "apto incluso para los menos iniciados" (suitable even for beginners) who are comfortable with the ~64 km distance.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that follow parts of the Ruta de Carlos V, the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), and the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Sur), among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect authentic Castilian steppe landscapes, with wide fields of cereal crops, undulating hills, and scattered rivers like the Pisuerga. The route combines rural tourism with historical heritage, including castles, churches, and traditional wine cellar districts, offering an environment without heavy traffic or crowds.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself focuses on cycling, the Palencia province is promoting cycling tourism combined with cultural and gastronomic experiences. Starting and ending in municipalities like Villamuriel de Cerrato, you can expect to find amenities such as cafes and restaurants. The region also features traditional "barrios de bodegas" (wine cellars districts) that might offer refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the ~64 km distance and moderate difficulty, you should bring adequate water and snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, sun protection, and a charged phone. While the region has low traffic, a helmet is always recommended for safety.
